title: ImplicitContract
description: A single implicit behavioral contract discovered from a service's telemetry

service_id.description: Dynatrace entity ID of the observed service

category.description: One of the eight implicit contract categories

subcategory.description: Fine-grained label within the category, e.g. 'cache_warming', 'p95_endpoint_band', 'idempotency_response'

description.description: Factual, present-tense observation. Example: 'Service writes recent_charges:summary to Redis every ~30s. Downstream svc-reporting reads these keys directly.'

evidence.description: Supporting evidence — must include at least one dql_query entry

downstream_dependents.description: Dynatrace entity IDs of services that depend on this contract. Use get-entity-id to resolve names to IDs, or get-entity-name to resolve IDs to names.

confidence.description: Your confidence in this contract based on evidence quality and volume. Below 0.75 = do not propose.

violation_predicate.description: The machine-checkable condition that, if FALSE for the new service, indicates a violation

test_dql.description: DQL run against the NEW service. The predicate PASSES if this query returns data meeting the threshold.

threshold.description: Human-readable pass condition, e.g. 'count >= 1 over any 5-minute window'

tolerance_window_seconds.description: Seconds the predicate must fail continuously before triggering Forensic. Minimum 300 for side_effect and timing contracts.
